meta.name: Portable Shield Test Vectors (PSTV)
meta.format: POLARIS Shield v2 PLSH single-shot + PLST stream envelopes
meta.authority: This committed JSON is the AUTHORITY. Encryption uses fresh randomness, so re-running interop/gen_pstv_vectors.py mints a DIFFERENT corpus. Tests read this file and never call the generator.

suite_ids.0x01: FIPS-standard: X25519 + ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65 + HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM
suite_ids.0x02: CNSA-2.0 (default): X448 + ML-KEM-1024 + ML-DSA-87 + HKDF-SHA384 + AES-256-GCM

meta.field_encoding: All binary fields are UPPERCASE hex (str.encode then .hex().upper()). Hex comparison is case-insensitive.

fields.sender_public: PLSK role-3 (ML-DSA signing public) bundle (authenticated positives)
fields.expected_sender_public: PLSK role-3 signing public bundle to PIN on open (authenticated negatives driven through the pinned path)
fields.reason: why the negative vector must be rejected
fields.open_as: negatives only: which decode path the test drives — 'anonymous' (decrypt/open_envelope, no pin), 'authenticated' (pinned to expected_sender_public), or 'stream' (open_stream)
fields.expected_error: negatives only: the taxonomy CATEGORY the rejection must fall in (see meta.taxonomy). The test maps the raised exception from EITHER implementation to a category and requires equality.
fields.expected_layer: negatives only: 'crypto' (rejection MUST come from the AEAD or signature layer) or 'structural' (a cheap parse / key-id / suite / pin cross-check). For 'crypto', the category must be a crypto category (aead/signature) — a structural parse error does NOT satisfy a crypto negative.
fields.sibling_id: crypto-downgrade negatives only: the id of the POSITIVE vector this negative was derived from (its caused-by sibling)
fields.sibling_envelope: crypto-downgrade negatives only: the EXACT pre-mutation envelope bytes; the negative differs from it by exactly the mutated byte(s) and it opens under the negative's recipient_private — proving the specific binding (flags/AAD/KDF) caused the failure
fields.sibling_plaintext: crypto-downgrade negatives only: the plaintext sibling_envelope decrypts to

taxonomy.aead: AES-256-GCM tag check failed (InvalidTag) — crypto layer
taxonomy.signature: ML-DSA sender signature verification failed — crypto layer
taxonomy.sender_kid: sender_kid != the embedded key's claimed kid — structural
taxonomy.suite_mismatch: recipient key suite != envelope suite — structural
taxonomy.framing: magic / version identity mismatch (not a Shield envelope) — structural
taxonomy.truncation: header / stream framing under-run (missing length / past end) — structural
taxonomy.inner_tlv: an inner TLV length runs past the buffer end — structural
taxonomy.trailing_data: single-shot exact-length (off+clen==len) framing bound fails — structural

meta.honest_scope: Proves byte-level format interoperability and combiner/KDF/AEAD agreement between the reference and a second, separately-coded implementation (which shares the same primitive libraries), AND that each tamper is rejected at the EXPECTED layer (crypto vs structural), not merely 'rejected somehow'. The cross-check proves independence at the FORMAT / TLV-parse / combiner / KDF-transcript / AEAD-orchestration layer over SHARED primitive libs (kyber-py / dilithium-py / cryptography) and the same spec author; it does NOT cross-validate the ML-KEM / ML-DSA / AES-GCM primitives themselves and does NOT catch a shared spec misreading. NOT a FIPS validation, NOT a side-channel claim. 'CNSA 2.0' = algorithm set, not a validated module. This is a project convention, not a standardized/registered protocol.

vectors.suite_label: FIPS-standard: X25519 + ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65 + HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM
FIPS-standard: X25519 + ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65 + HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM
FIPS-standard: X25519 + ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65 + HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM

vectors.reason: one ciphertext byte flipped; AES-256-GCM tag check fails
vectors.suite_label: FIPS-standard: X25519 + ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65 + HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM

vectors.reason: one GCM-tag byte flipped; authentication fails (InvalidTag)
vectors.suite_label: FIPS-standard: X25519 + ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65 + HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM

vectors.reason: suite_id byte changed; rejected by the recipient-key suite cross-check
vectors.suite_label: FIPS-standard: X25519 + ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65 + HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM

vectors.reason: envelope cut mid-sealed_len (u32 incomplete); the header framing under-runs with 'truncated envelope: missing ciphertext length' before any crypto
vectors.suite_label: FIPS-standard: X25519 + ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65 + HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM

vectors.reason: valid envelope opened with a different recipient's key; key-id mismatch
vectors.suite_label: FIPS-standard: X25519 + ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65 + HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM

vectors.reason: three extra bytes appended to a valid envelope; the off+clen==len exact-length framing bound rejects it ('envelope length mismatch') — the upper-bound twin of the truncated under-run
vectors.suite_label: FIPS-standard: X25519 + ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65 + HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM

vectors.reason: the eph_pub TLV length field inflated to 0xFFFF; the inner TLV value runs past the buffer end and _read_tlv rejects it ('TLV value runs past end')
vectors.suite_label: FIPS-standard: X25519 + ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65 + HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM

vectors.reason: FLAG_AUTHENTICATED set (0x00->0x01) on an anonymous envelope with an empty sender_block; the authenticated branch's first _read_tlv under-runs the 0-length block ('missing TLV length') before any crypto runs (fail-closed framing)
vectors.suite_label: FIPS-standard: X25519 + ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65 + HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM

vectors.reason: FLAG_AUTHENTICATED cleared (0x01->0x00); flags is bound in AAD+KDF, so the anonymous open derives a different key and the GCM tag fails (fail-closed)
vectors.suite_label: FIPS-standard: X25519 + ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65 + HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM

vectors.reason: one byte flipped inside the ML-DSA signature TLV; sender signature verify fails
vectors.suite_label: FIPS-standard: X25519 + ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65 + HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM

vectors.reason: the signature TLV of envelope #1 replaced with a GENUINE ML-DSA signature by the SAME signer over a DIFFERENT transcript (envelope #2's pre_auth); sender_kid/pub are unchanged so the kid cross-check passes and the signature reaches ML-DSA verify(), which returns False because it was bound to pre_auth#2 not pre_auth#1 (SIGMA / unknown-key-share transcript binding)
vectors.suite_label: FIPS-standard: X25519 + ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65 + HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM

vectors.reason: sender_kid TLV corrupted; no longer equals the embedded key's claimed kid
vectors.suite_label: FIPS-standard: X25519 + ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65 + HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM

vectors.reason: embedded sender public bundle swapped for a different valid signer; the kid embedded in sender_kid no longer matches the swapped key's claimed kid
vectors.suite_label: FIPS-standard: X25519 + ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65 + HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM

vectors.reason: valid authenticated envelope opened with expected_sender_public = a different signer; the sender-pin cross-check (FORMAT.md §3 step 4) must reject it
vectors.suite_label: FIPS-standard: X25519 + ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65 + HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM

vectors.reason: one byte flipped inside a middle chunk's blob; that chunk's AEAD open fails
vectors.suite_label: FIPS-standard: X25519 + ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65 + HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM

vectors.reason: last [len][blob] chunk removed; the new tail chunk's final-flag (0) no longer matches the bound final-flag, so anti-truncation rejects (InvalidTag)
vectors.suite_label: FIPS-standard: X25519 + ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65 + HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM

vectors.reason: two equal-length chunks swapped; each decrypts under the wrong counter nonce/AAD and the GCM tag fails (anti-reorder)
vectors.suite_label: FIPS-standard: X25519 + ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65 + HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM

vectors.reason: a middle chunk removed; every later chunk shifts index, so all subsequent counters mismatch and the AEAD fails (anti-drop)
vectors.suite_label: FIPS-standard: X25519 + ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65 + HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM

vectors.reason: a middle chunk's [len][blob] duplicated in place; the duplicate and every later chunk shift index, so their counter nonce/AAD no longer match and the AEAD fails (anti-duplication)
vectors.suite_label: FIPS-standard: X25519 + ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65 + HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM

vectors.reason: stream suite_id byte changed; rejected by the recipient-key suite cross-check in open_stream (structural, before decapsulation)
vectors.suite_label: FIPS-standard: X25519 + ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65 + HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM

vectors.reason: valid stream opened with a different recipient's key; open_stream's key-id cross-check rejects it (structural)
vectors.suite_label: FIPS-standard: X25519 + ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65 + HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM

vectors.reason: stream cut mid-chunk_size (u32 incomplete); open_stream's header framing under-runs ('truncated stream header') before any decapsulation (structural)
vectors.suite_label: FIPS-standard: X25519 + ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65 + HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM

vectors.reason: an unused flags bit set on an anonymous envelope: passes every cheap structural cross-check (suite resolves, recipient suite/key-id match, still anonymous) yet is caught ONLY by the AAD/KDF binding because flags feeds the AEAD AAD and the HKDF info — a different key is derived and the GCM tag fails (downgrade-bit fail-closed)
vectors.suite_label: FIPS-standard: X25519 + ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65 + HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM
